name: Route from Lérida to the Santa Ana
name_original: Embalse de Santa Ana desde Lérida
description: Cycle 41 miles to Santa Ana Reservoir from Lerida, a moderate touring route with 1397 feet of gain, offering scenic views and rural charm.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Segria offers diverse terrain, from flat, open plains to the foothills of the Pyrenees. The region is characterized by rich agricultural landscapes, including extensive farmlands and fruit orchards, particularly along the Canal d'Urgell. Routes often follow canals and rivers, providing accessible paths that connect local villages and offer views of dry hills and agricultural fields. Quiet, well-maintained roads make Segria a suitable destination for various cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Segria The most popular touring cycling route is Utxesa Reservoir from Aitona — Circular through Segrià, a 18.6 miles (30.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 6 minutes to complete. This route offers a circular path around the Utxesa Reservoir, traversing agricultural areas. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Catalan Way: Lleida and Alcoletge Loop, a moderate 19.3 miles (31.0 km) path. This loop explores the areas around Lleida and Alcoletge, often passing through the region's characteristic farmlands. Local touring cyclists also love the Rufea - Montoliu - Puigverd de Lleida - Artesa — circular by the Canal de Urgell, a 23.7 miles (38.1 km) trail leading through agricultural landscapes alongside the Canal de Urgell, often completed in about 2 hours 59 minutes. Touring cycling around Segria is defined by diverse terrain, agricultural heartlands, and routes along canals and rivers. The network offers options for various ability levels, from flat paths to more challenging climbs in the foothills. The routes in Segria are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 140 reviews. More than 2400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Segria's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Segria?
A: Segria offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 350 options available on komoot. These routes cater to various preferences, from easy rides to more challenging excursions.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Segria?
A: You'll find diverse terrain in Segria, ranging from flat, open plains ideal for relaxed rides to more challenging climbs in the foothills of the Pyrenees. The region is characterized by rich agricultural landscapes, extensive farmlands, and fruit orchards, particularly along the Canal d'Urgell. Many routes follow canals and rivers, offering accessible paths with views of dry hills and agricultural fields.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Segria?
A: The region offers a generally pleasant environment for cycling. Spring, particularly March and April, is especially scenic due to the 'almond blossom tourism' and the flowering of apple and pear trees, adding a vibrant and fragrant dimension to your rides.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Segria has numerous easy routes, with approximately 45 touring cycling options classified as easy. These often follow flat plains, canals, and rivers, providing accessible paths suitable for all skill levels, including families. A good example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33933817" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rufea Wetlands – Tòfol Footbridge loop from Alcarràs&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a relatively flat experience.

Q: Are there more challenging touring cycling routes in Segria?
A: For those seeking a greater challenge, Segria offers around 114 difficult touring cycling routes. These routes often venture into the foothills of the Pyrenees, providing more significant elevation gains and descents. An example of a longer route with more elevation is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970589362" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route from Lérida to the Santa Ana&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical or cultural sights can I see along touring cycling routes in Segria?
A: Segria is rich in historical and cultural sites. You can combine your cycling with visits to landmarks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4606595" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Paeria Palace&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4972766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Seu Vella de Lleida&lt;/a&gt; (Old Cathedral), and various historic towns and villages with watchtowers and castles. Many rural tracks lead directly to these cultural stops.

Q: Are there natural attractions or scenic viewpoints on the touring cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The region's agricultural landscapes, canals, and rivers provide continuous scenic views. You can cycle along the Canal d'Urgell, explore areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5581211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parc de la Mitjana&lt;/a&gt;, or cross the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5187758" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tòfol Footbridge&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5182115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mitjana Iron Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The seasonal blooms in spring also offer a stunning natural spectacle.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes in Segria?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Segria are designed as circular paths, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6955526" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tòfol Footbridge – La Paeria Palace loop from Torres de Segre&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10469725" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tòfol Footbridge – Utxesa Reservoir loop from Lleida-Pirineus&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about cycling in Segria?
A: The touring cycling routes in Segria are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 140 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the tranquil agricultural landscapes, and the well-maintained, quiet roads that make for an enjoyable touring experience.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the touring cycling routes?
A: Segria's touring cycling routes often connect local villages and pass through historic towns. These areas typically offer amenities such as cafes, pubs, and various accommodation options, allowing you to refuel and rest during or after your rides.

Q: Are there routes that pass through the city of Lleida?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate or start near Lleida, allowing you to explore the urban and surrounding areas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6956039" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tòfol Footbridge – La Paeria Palace loop from Lleida-Pirineus&lt;/a&gt; is a great option that begins in the city.

Q: Are there routes that follow canals or rivers?
A: Many routes in Segria utilize the region's extensive canal system, such as the Canal d'Urgell, and follow river paths. These provide flat, accessible, and scenic cycling experiences. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31825161" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tòfol Footbridge – Canal Path Near Lleida loop from Lleida-Pirineus&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent example of a route that follows a canal path.
tips: View over the Santa Ana reservoir. A good vantage point on one side of the Santa Ana dam and on the other the Alfarrås plain.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of cycling this route?
faqs.answer: The route from Lleida to the Santa Ana Reservoir is rated as moderate. It requires good fitness, as while the overall elevation gain is manageable, some sections may present steeper climbs, especially as you approach the Pre-Pyrenees region.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and surface can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The route largely consists of paved surfaces, making it suitable for road cycling and touring bikes. However, be prepared for some unpaved segments. For these sections, a more versatile bike like a gravel bike or good fitness might be needed. Road conditions are generally good but can vary.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the way?
faqs.answer: The journey offers diverse landscapes, transitioning from the plains of Lleida towards the Pre-Pyrenees. You'll enjoy views over the water and the Alfarràs plain, with the submerged old town of Tragó de Noguera adding a unique historical element. The route also showcases Romanesque and Gothic architecture, as well as mountain ridges with distinctive gypsum folds. The main highlight is the Santa Ana Reservoir and Dam itself.

faqs.question: Is the Santa Ana Reservoir from Lerida route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and length of nearly 66 kilometers with some elevation gain and potentially unpaved sections, this route is generally not recommended for beginners or young families. It requires good fitness and some cycling experience.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region generally offers pleasant conditions for cycling outside of the hottest summer months. Spring and autumn are often ideal, providing comfortable temperatures and beautiful scenery. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route from Lerida?
faqs.answer: As the route starts from Lleida, you would typically look for parking within the city. There are various public parking options available in Lleida, both paid and free, depending on your exact starting point within the city. Consider parking near the city's outskirts for easier access to the route.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the cycling route?
faqs.answer: While there are no specific restrictions against dogs on public roads and paths in this region, it's important to ensure your dog is well-behaved and kept under control, especially when cycling. Always be mindful of local regulations and other trail users.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to cycle to the Santa Ana Reservoir?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route to the Santa Ana Reservoir. It uses public roads and paths, making it freely accessible.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of "El Camí IT2TR1 El Camí de Ponent: de Balaguer a Montserrat" (10% of the route), "La Litera. BTT. Ruta 2. Yesos y Embalse de Santa Ana" (7%), and "Camí de la Plana" (10%).

faqs.question: What should I pack for a touring cycling trip to Santa Ana Reservoir?
faqs.answer: For this moderate touring cycling route, you should pack essentials like water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate cycling attire, sun protection, and a first-aid kit. Given the potential for varying terrain, a versatile bike and suitable tires are also advisable.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or water stops along the route?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through rural areas, you may find small villages or towns along the way where you can replenish water or find a cafe. However, it's always best to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for longer stretches between towns, as facilities might be sparse.
